propositions: Experiment A measures self-consistency of the existing Pass-2/Pass-3 judge by re-judging N=50 frozen findings from the G6/E4/T3 cohort with 3 votes each, computing Fleiss' kappa and raw agreement over the decision field.
Experiment A passes only if kappa >= 0.6 AND raw agreement >= 0.8; a sub-floor result is recorded as an R5 blocker.

Experiment B measures order-shuffle stability of the gate verdict over 14 committed plans with >=3 top-level sections, each judged over 3 distinct section-order permutations via review_plan against a throwaway store clone.
Experiment B passes only if kappa >= 0.6 AND raw agreement >= 0.8; a sub-floor result is recorded as an R3 prerequisite failure.

Infra-INDETERMINATE verdicts carrying coverage.llm_unavailable or coverage.verify_failed are execution failures that are dropped and re-run, while genuine judge-INDETERMINATE is kept as its own agreement category.
E6 measures reliability (repeat-agreement) not accuracy against gold, never treating judge output as truth, and makes no src/rebar behavior changes.
